Confirmed verbatim: alendronate tablets (Fosamax), ibandronate (Boniva), and risedronate (Actonel) all appear in the Preferred column with a 'ql' (quantity limit) superscript and no 'cc' (clinical-criteria/PA) marker. Non-preferred items -- alendronate solution (Fosamax Solution), risedronate DR (Atelvia), Binosto, and Fosamax Plus D -- appear in the 'Non-Preferred / Requires Prior Authorization' column per the document's own column header. records.verificationNote: Read the entire 33-page Maryland Department of Health Office of Pharmacy Services Medicaid Pharmacy Program Preferred Drug List, effective 7/1/2026, fetched from health.maryland.gov/mmcp/pap/docs/PDL-7.1.2026-Final-v5.pdf. Zoledronic acid, Reclast, and Zometa do not appear anywhere in the document, including the 'Bone Resorption Suppression and Related Agents' row on page 19 that covers every other bone-osteoporosis agent in this gather. This is a genuine, confirmed absence from the pharmacy PDL rather than a failed search -- it is consistent with the common national pattern of physician-administered IV infusions (billed under the medical benefit / buy-and-bill) being excluded from retail-pharmacy PDLs entirely, since the PDL only governs drugs dispensed through the pharmacy benefit. No PDL-level preferred/non-preferred designation, PA requirement, or tier statement could be confirmed either way for this drug, so the preferred-agent fields above describe the standard clinical/dosing facts for zoledronic acid rather than a value read off this document, and verification is marked 'partial' rather than 'verified' or 'example' -- the correct, current, on-topic source was read in full; it simply does not address this specific drug.

records.verificationNote: The osteoporosis-dose denosumab product (Prolia, 60 mg/mL) is not confirmed in either fetched Kaiser MAS document: the primary 'District of Columbia, Maryland, and Virginia Marketplace Formulary' (Last Update: 06/02/2026, all 47 pages checked) and the companion 'Specialty Drugs List' (effective 04/07/2026, all 19 pages checked, itself fetched via direct curl since WebFetch's 10MB size cap rejected it) both omit Prolia/denosumab-60mg entirely, while carrying the related 120 mg oncology-dose denosumab products (XGEVA plus two biosimilars) on the specialty list.
